Description: We are seeking a seasoned Senior Business Development Manager to join one of the most impactful teams at Anomalo. This role focuses on developing and executing co-sell strategies with major technology partners to drive annual recurring revenue and enhance Anomalo's market presence. This person will be tasked with having an in-depth understanding of the sales and product organizations at some of our most strategic partners, such as Snowflake and Databricks. They will be the primary point of contact for all partnership activities, enabling them on Anomalo’s product and establishing a mutual value proposition for the partnership across go-to-market and product teams. The role requires an individual with the experience, interpersonal skills, and energy to operate in a wide spectrum of situations. They will be an enthusiastic and motivated individual who is visibly passionate and can attract and form lasting relationships. This position will report to the Director of Business Development. What you’ll do as the Senior Business Development Manager at Anomalo: Effectively manage partner relationships, developing rapport with key decision makers, and serve as a primary point of contact for all partnership activities Identify and support GTM opportunities with partners by aligning with their goals and roadmaps to drive Anomalo’s overall strategy and expansion goals Drive ARR growth through effective partnership frameworks-including developing comprehensive co-sell strategies that align with both Anomalo's and partners' goals Work Cross-Functionally with Anomalo marketing, sales, and customer success teams to ensure that GTM strategies are integrated across all aspects of Anomalo's operations Understand partner and Anomalo roadmaps to identify net new points of integration, and work with both Anomalo and the partner’s product and engineering teams on scoping ROI and seeing these to fruition What you'll bring to the team: Over 8 years of professional experience, with 5+ years in partner or account development in the software/technology industry Experience managing joint GTM activities with global companies, including development and tracking of joint sell-with programs Knowledge of the Modern Data Stack and experience with platforms such as Snowflake, Databricks, and Google Strong written, oral and social communication skills Focus on execution Experience working cross-functionally is required Strong ability to influence without direct control Ideal candidates will also have: Experience owning partnerships with Snowflake or Databricks or hyperscalers such as Google Enterprise sales experience Salary range: The estimated on-target earnings for this role is $200,000 - $220,000, plus meaningful equity Travel: Planned travel once per quarter to spend time with the team is required Ability to travel (up to 25%) in support of the above responsibilities

Who We Are Atlan is building the missing context layer for data and AI, helping enterprises close the AI value chasm. Today, 95% of AI pilots fail because AI systems don’t understand the context behind data: what it means, how it’s governed, and how it should be used. Atlan connects to every part of the modern data and AI stack to unify this context into a single, shared layer that both humans and AI agents can rely on. With Atlan, teams can discover, understand, and trust their data; build and collaborate on a shared body of knowledge; and activate that context across analytics, operations, and AI workflows.Trusted by global enterprises like Mastercard, Workday, General Motors, Unilever, Ralph Lauren, FOX, Nasdaq, and Medtronic , we’re backed by world-class investors including GIC, Insight Partners, Meritech, Peak XV, and Salesforce Ventures Why this role matters 🧭 Atlan is one of the fastest-growing companies in the modern data and AI stack — trusted by the world’s largest enterprises and loved by a community of more than 140,000 data practitioners. In one of the noisiest categories in tech, we consistently punch above our weight. From unforgettable multi-channel experiences at industry events like Gartner’s D&A Summit, Snowflake Summit, and Databricks’ Data+AI Summit, to digital campaigns that shape how teams think about data and AI, Atlan’s creativity and execution make us stand out. We’re now looking for a Creative Director to shape the future of how Atlan shows up to the world — evolving our brand identity for the AI era and building the creative systems that help us lead an entire category. ✨ See how Atlan showed up at Snowflake Summit 2025 → Link to LinkedIn posts Your mission 🚀 As our Creative Director, you’ll lead and inspire our in-house creative team, and agency partners, to bring Atlan’s story to life across every touchpoint — digital, physical, and experiential. You’ll combine visionary thinking with hands-on craft, using AI to accelerate exploration while applying creative judgment to produce work that cuts through the noise and punches above its weight. From reimagining our presence at major industry events to building scalable systems that empower Atlanians to bring our brand to life at scale, your leadership will define how Atlan’s brand evolves and scales in the AI era. What you’ll do 💡 Lead a multidisciplinary in-house team and external agencies across brand, digital, and experiential design. Set and evolve the creative vision for Atlan’s brand in the AI era — using AI for rapid ideation and production while ensuring every output meets a high bar of originality and craft. Own the brand system: visual identity, guidelines, and scalable frameworks that empower teams company-wide. Run Atlan’s in-house creative studio, delivering campaign and event assets that stop the scroll, spark emotion, and reflect world-class creative judgment. Partner across teams — Growth, Field Marketing, Content, Product Marketing, Sales Enablement, and the Executive team — to translate strategy into storytelling. Elevate our presence across every surface, ensuring every experience cuts through the noise and communicates Atlan’s brand positioning. Lead creative direction for major industry events, ensuring Atlan stands out for our data and AI mission. Champion craft, feedback discipline, and creative bravery — inspiring bold ideas and flawless execution. What you bring 🎨 8+ years in design or creative leadership roles, with 4+ years leading creative teams or agencies. Expertise across brand, digital, campaign, and experiential design. Hands-on fluency in Adobe Creative Cloud, Figma, and modern creative and AI-enabled tools. Demonstrated ability to use AI to accelerate creative workflows while applying rigorous human judgment to refine and elevate the output. Proven ability to translate business strategy into world-class creative storytelling. Experience building scalable brand systems that balance creativity with consistency. Collabor

You will shape how the world understands Atlan. Own Product Marketing Strategy and Outcomes Set the Direction: Own product marketing strategy at Atlan. Lead a Product Marketing Squad Build and Lead the Team: Lead, mentor, and develop a small squad of product marketers. Multiply Impact: Ensure the team produces high-quality narrative assets like messaging frameworks, playbooks, and launch kits that enable teams across the company to move faster and stay aligned. 3. Architect Strategic Platform Narratives Own the Core Story: Define and evolve Atlan’s core platform narrative, including personas, proof points, and hooks across Tier 1 launches and flagship GTM moments. Make It Scalable: Build narratives that are durable and reusable, powering everything from sales conversations to website copy to keynote launches. 4. Drive Deep Market Insight and Differentiated Messaging Know the Market Deeply: Lead continuous customer and market research to understand buyers across technical and executive personas. Turn Complexity into Clarity: Translate complex data and AI concepts into simple, compelling, and clearly differentiated value for the market. 5. Orchestrate Cross-Functional GTM Execution Lead Launch Excellence: Guide GTM strategy and execution for major launches, campaigns, and events from early planning through execution. Partner Creatively: Work closely with Design and Content to push for clarity, creativity, and quality across all customer-facing experiences. Enable Revenue Teams: Ensure sales and revenue teams are fluent in Atlan’s story through thoughtful, high-impact enablement programs. 💡 What Makes You a Match for Us? Senior Product Marketing Leader: 10+ years of product marketing experience

About Boomi and What Makes Us Special

Are you ready to work at a fast-growing company where you can make a difference? Boomi aims to make the world a better place by connecting everyone to everything, anywhere. Our award-winning, intelligent integration and automation platform helps organizations power the future of business. At Boomi, you’ll work with world-class people and industry-leading technology. We hire trailblazers with an entrepreneurial spirit who can solve challenging problems, make a real impact, and want to be part of building something big. If this sounds like a good fit for you, check out boomi.com  or visit our Boomi Careers page to learn more.

How You’ll Make An Impact

As an AI Forward Deployed Engineer, you will be the hands-on expert responsible for turning a customer's agentic vision into a reality. In this critical role, you will engage with the customer team to ideate use cases and create demos/POCs.  Assisting the Sales & COE team, you will scope the platform work and partner with Professional Services to scope the implementation work.

Post-Sale, your mission is to execute the Agentic "Solution Blueprint" defined during the sales cycle, ensuring customers achieve their first major value milestone as quickly and efficiently as possible.

What You’ll Do

  • Identify Use Cases: Work with the presales SC to identify and refine future use cases for each customer based on implementation insights.

  • Create Demos / POCs: Work with the customer team to identify key proof points and build out a demo/POC that successfully shows how the Boomi platform delivers.

  • Lead Implementation: Develop a detailed project plan for the customer implementation, based on the agent's predefined steps and the Solution Blueprint.

  • Provide Hands-on Expertise: Act as the primary technical point of contact during onboarding, providing hands-on guidance and project management for the setup, configuration, and integration required by the agent.

  • Drive to First Value: Proactively manage the project to achieve the first significant value milestone, ensuring the customer sees a tangible return on their investment.

  • Execute a Successful Transition: Manage the project closeout and execute a smooth transition of the customer to their long-term Customer Success Manager (CSM) and Professional Services for ongoing support and expansion.

The Experience You Bring

  • Experience with cloud-native services and infrastructure across AWS, Azure, or GCP.

Business Area: Sales Engineering Seniority Level: Mid-Senior level Job Description: At Cloudera, we empower people to transform complex data into clear and actionable insights. With as much data under management as the hyperscalers, we're the preferred data partner for the top companies in almost every industry.  Powered by the relentless innovation of the open source community, Cloudera advances digital transformation for the world’s largest enterprises. We are looking for a highly technical, customer-facing AI Solutions Engineer to act as the tip of the spear for our enterprise go-to-market motion. This is not a "slideware" or pure presentation role. You will embed with prospective clients, rolling up your sleeves to architect, build, and deploy end-to-end Data, ML, and Generative AI proofs-of-concept (PoCs) and Minimum Viable Products (MVPs). You will leverage the best paradigms of the modern data stack—combining the streaming and hybrid-security prowess of the Cloudera ecosystem with the unified Lakehouse and AI/ML developer experience ecosystem. Your ultimate goal is to prove technical value through working code, turning complex business problems into production-ready AI applications that secure the technical win. As an AI Solutions Engineer, you will: Prototyping: Lead hands-on technical engagements. Build custom, production-grade AI/ML prototypes, RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipelines, and data ingestion workflows directly in the customer's environment to prove business value. Pre-Sales Technical Leadership: Act as the primary technical advisor during the sales cycle. Lead deep-dive architectural whiteboarding sessions, handle technical objections, and translate ambiguous business needs into concrete, scalable data architectures. Lakehouse Architecture Design: Architect modern, open data lakehouses. Design pipelines that stream data via Apache NiFi or Flink into open table formats (Apache Iceberg or Delta Lake), optimizing for both BI and AI workloads. Cloudera is looking for an Engineering Manager in Hungary with a strong technical background to help drive the development of Cloudera’s Operational Database components and services. The Operational Database team is responsible for the development of Cloudera Operational Database as well as Apache HBase, Phoenix, ZooKeeper and related open source projects. Cloudera Operational Database (aka. COD) is a public cloud based solution based on Apache HBase and Phoenix HBase is the Hadoop database, a distributed, scalable, big data store and is one of the most widely used components in Hadoop, an essential tool for data engineering. Phoenix adds an SQL flavour to HBase, combining the SQL world with the scalability of HBase. ZooKeeper is the high availability coordination service of the Big Data world and is one of the most widely used components in the Hadoop ecosystem, an essential tool for coordinating component instances in large clusters. As an Engineering Manager, you will be leading the Hungary based development team and together with its USA and APAC-based peer teams you will develop, distribute and support COD. You will also lead the team that develops open source HBase, Phoenix and ZooKeeper. You will help plan and deliver the innovation roadmap, make our products more supportable, enterprise ready and feature-rich. As Engineering Manager you will: Lead a team of strong engineers to design, code, and implement elegant, scalable, enterprise-quality application services. Background in a distributed database systems, including database storage internals, NoSQL storage. Strong verbal and written communication skills in English Solid interpersonal skills conducive to a distributed team environment Solid experience with system software design and development with a strong understanding of computer architecture, storage, network, and IO subsystems, and distributed systems Strong Understanding of Distributed Systems and building systems at scale. This role serves as a senior technical authority and the primary owner of Security Architecture Reviews (SARs), responsible for evaluating complex designs and defining security standards across network, identity, application, data, and AI domains. In addition to core security architecture responsibilities, this role will define security guardrails for AI coding agents, LLM integrations, and autonomous assistants, partnering closely with IT Operations, Product Security, and Data Science teams to ensure innovation is delivered in a secure, risk-aware manner. The Staff Security Architect operates as a technical leader and mentor, translating architectural decisions into clear risk outcomes and durable security patterns. As a Sr. Staff Security Architect, you will: Security Architecture & Strategy: Lead the design and evolution of security architecture across hybrid environments, ensuring security by design is embedded into on-prem, cloud, application, data, and AI-enabled systems. Security Architecture Reviews (SARs): Serve as the primary authority for Security Architecture Reviews, evaluating new platforms, services, vendors, and architectural changes to identify material risk and define secure design patterns aligned with enterprise standards. Cloud & Platform Security: Define and maintain a consistent security posture across AWS, Azure, and GCP, including identity architecture, network segmentation, workload isolation, logging, and secure integration with on-premises environments. Application & Data Security: Assess application architectures and data flows to identify security risks related to access control, data protection, trust boundaries, and dependency management, including modern cloud-native and AI-enabled applications. AI Security & Risk Governance: Define security guardrails for AI-enabled capabilities, including LLM integrations, coding assistants, and automation platforms. Conduct targeted risk assessments focused on data exposure, misuse, and model interaction risks as part of broader architecture reviews. Zero Trust & Identity Architecture: Lead identity and access architecture initiatives, including Zero Trust principles and lifecycle management for human and non-human identities, ensuring consistent enforcement across hybrid environments. Threat Modeling & Risk Analysis: Drive threat modeling and architectural risk analysis for complex systems, identifying cross-domain risks spanning network, identity, application, cloud, and AI components. Security Automation & Guardrails: Promote security-as-code approaches by defining and enforcing architectural guardrails through automation (e.g., Terraform, policy-as-code, Python) to ensure consistency and scalability. Technical Leadership & Mentorship: Act as a senior technical leader and mentor, influencing engineering teams through expertise, design guidance, and risk-based decision-making rather than direct authority. Policy Translation & Standards: Translate regulatory and framework requirements (e.g., NIST AI RMF, ISO 27001) into clear security requirements and enforceable architectural standards. We are excited about you if you have: Experience: 10+ years of progressive experience in cybersecurity, with at least 5 years specifically in a Security Architect or Staff-level capacity.

Communication & Presentation : Demonstrable ability to create and deliver compelling technical presentations and live product demos to a wide range of audiences, from engineers

Remote Role At Cloudera, our Data Services Pillar is the heart of data innovation. We don’t just work with technology; we build it. Our mission is to empower data practitioners by creating seamless, enterprise-grade experiences for data engineering, warehousing, streaming, operational databases, and AI. Cloudera is looking for an exceptional and passionate software engineer to join the Data Warehouse engineering team. The technology stack includes popular open source query engines - Apache Hive, Impala, Trino, and table formats like Apache Iceberg. Thus, there is ample scope for collaboration and contribution to open source. This is an exciting opportunity to work on products that handle complex SQL query workloads on public or private clouds as part of the Cloudera Data Platform (CDP). As a Senior Software Engineer, you will: Work on large-scale, distributed systems to help drive Hive innovation and build additional components around it to enhance the Hive ecosystem. Have an exciting opportunity to work on products that handle complex SQL query workloads on public or private clouds as part of Cloudera Data Platform (CDP). Design and develop features for parallel and distributed query engines to help drive innovation in CDP. Focus on query optimization, performance and scalability of SQL queries. Write design documentation for key features and capabilities. Improve code quality through writing tests, automation, and code reviews. Understand the customer’s workload and provide effective technical solutions. We are excited about you if you have: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science or equivalent, and 6 years of experience. Experience with query optimization using tools like Apache Calcite. Clean coding habits, attention to detail, and a focus on quality. Hands-on programmer with strong data structures and algorithms skills. Java experience is desired. Good understanding of database internals, query processing and SQL query optimization. Strong oral and written communication skills. Ability to work effectively on cross-functional projects. You may also have: Experience with contributing to any of the open-source Apache projects like Hive, Impala, Calcite or an RDBMS. Experience with the Hadoop ecosystem and file formats like Parquet, ORC. Experience with public cloud infrastructures such as Microsoft Azure, Amazon Web Services and Google Cloud Platform. Recognized contributions to open source projects. Why this role matters: This is your opportunity to build cloud-native solutions that are deployable anywhere whether in massive clusters on any cloud provider or in private data centers. You’ll work with cutting-edge technologies like Trino, Spark, Airflow, and advanced AI inferencing systems to shape the future of analytics. Your code will directly influence how data engineers, analysts, and developers worldwide find value in their data. We believe in the power of open source. You’ll collaborate with project committers, contributing upstream to keep technologies like Apache Hive and Impala evolving. You’ll harden these engines for rock-solid security, optimize them for peak performance, and make them effortlessly run across all environments.  Join us and help build the trusted, cloud-native platform that powers insights for the most data-intensive companies on the planet. Joining Collibra’s Strategy and Operations team

We're looking for a BI & Analytics Manager to own customer lifecycle analytics within our Analytics & Insights team, part of Collibra's Strategy function. You'll be responsible for understanding how customers adopt, expand, and succeed with Collibra's platform, and for building the analytical foundations that make those insights scalable, governed, and increasingly AI-assisted.

This role sits at the intersection of strategic analytics and the future of how Collibra consumes its own data. You'll own a critical analytics domain end-to-end, from customer health and product adoption through expansion patterns and renewal dynamics. But you'll also help shape how we govern the data and semantic models that underpin those insights, and how we move toward AI-powered self-service analytics. We're building Collibra to be its own first customer for data governance and AI-assisted discovery, and this role is part of that ambition.

You'll report to the Director, Analytics and Insights and be the dedicated analytics partner for our Services leadership, and a key contributor to the cross-functional diagnostic frameworks our executive committee relies on. You'll work within a team that's building a unified, AI-ready data foundation, partnering closely with our Data Office, cross-functional counterparts in RevOps and Product, and senior stakeholders who expect data-driven counsel, not just data delivery.

If you're energized by customer lifecycle analytics, building governed and AI-ready data products, and the opportunity to shape how a data governance company does analytics internally, we want to talk.

This is a hybrid role based in our Brussels office. Our hybrid model means you’ll work from the office at least two days each week. This setup helps us stay connected, work more closely together, and keep making progress as a team.

BI & Analytics Managers at Collibra are responsible for

  • Own customer lifecycle analytics: health scoring, adoption patterns, expansion signals, renewal dynamics, and everything in between
  • Be the analytics partner for Services leadership. Understand their priorities, shape the agenda, and bring insights before they're asked for
  • Build diagnostic frameworks that connect product usage to commercial outcomes so leadership can see where customers get stuck or succeed
  • Lead analysis from start to finish: gather data, build models, test hypotheses about customer behavior, and communicate findings clearly to stakeholders
  • Feed insights into executive reporting with context and narrative, not just numbers
  • Work with the Foundation team on the unified customer and product data model. Make sure your domain is well-represented and well-documented
  • Champion data governance best practices within the analytics team: maintain clear data documentation, metric definitions, and lineage for your domain, contributing to the semantic layer that enables AI-assisted analytics
  • Push the boundaries of our AI analytics tooling. We're already using AI-assisted analysis and we need someone who'll keep finding new ways to make it work harder for the team and for stakeholders
  • Use the Collibra platform as part of your daily workflow to document, govern, and share the data products you create. You'll help us understand our own product through the lens of a power user
  • Work with cross-functional partners in RevOps, Marketing, and Product to connect the dots across acquisition, adoption, expansion, and renewal

You have

  • 5+ years of experience in analytics, business intelligence, or a related field, with a meaningful portion spent on customer success, lifecycle, or post-sale analytics in a SaaS environment.
  • Strong SQL skills and experience working with complex data models sp

Zilliz is a fast-growing startup developing the industry’s leading vector database company for enterprise-grade AI. Founded by the engineers behind Milvus, the world’s most popular open-source vector database, the company builds next-generation database technologies to help organizations quickly create AI applications. On a mission to democratize AI, Zilliz is committed to simplifying data management for AI applications and making vector databases accessible to every organization.


Zilliz is looking for an early-career engineer to join our team as an Open Source Support Engineer and grow into a technical expert while helping developers build applications with Milvus, the leading open-source vector database.

Milvus powers vector search for thousands of developers worldwide. By helping open-source users succeed in real production environments, this role directly contributes to the growth of the Milvus ecosystem, while providing critical feedback that shapes the product’s evolution.

This is an excellent opportunity for recent graduates or engineers early in their careers who are excited to solve real infrastructure problems, work directly with users, and develop deep expertise in distributed systems and vector search technology.
